summary: Iteration 10 — brute-force lockout (5-attempt threshold + 15-min lock) and password reset flow (1hr TTL, single-use, lockout-clearing) end-to-end validated. Backend: 16/16 new tests + 37/37 regression (test_csrf_cookie_auth + test_farmer_regression) = 53/53 PASS. Frontend E2E: /forgot-password, /reset-password (with and without token), Login -> Forgot link, full forgot->log-parse->reset->login round-trip, and 5-strike UI lockout with progressive 'N attempts remaining' toasts followed by 'Too many failed attempts...' all pass. No defects found in this iteration.

minor.issue: On the 5th wrong attempt (the one that TRIGGERS the lockout), the 429 response does NOT carry the Retry-After header — that header is only emitted on the 6th and subsequent locked-out requests (because check_lockout is the only place that adds it, and on the triggering 5th request check_lockout has already returned). The spec asks for Retry-After on every 429. Low impact (UX nit only); a single-line fix would be to add `headers={'Retry-After': str(LOCK_DURATION.total_seconds())}` to the 429 raise inside login() when remaining hits 0.

passed: Brute-force: attempts 1-2 -> 401 'Invalid credentials'; attempt 3 -> 401 with '(2 attempts remaining)'; attempt 4 -> 401 with '(1 attempt remaining)'; attempt 5 -> 429 lockout; attempt 6 -> 429 + Retry-After header
During lockout, correct password is also rejected with 429 for the same ip+email
Successful login clears the failed-attempt doc in db.login_attempts
Per-{ip}:{email} isolation — locking one email leaves a different email still able to log in
/api/auth/forgot-password with EXISTING email -> 200 generic message + PASSWORD_RESET_LINK logged + new row in db.password_reset_tokens (used=false, expires_at ~now+1h)
/api/auth/forgot-password with NON-EXISTENT email -> 200 same generic message; no token row created (no enumeration leak)
/api/auth/forgot-password is CSRF-exempt — bare POST with no cookies/no X-CSRF-Token still returns 200
/api/auth/reset-password full happy path: valid token + new_password>=6 -> 200; bcrypt hash updated; token marked used=true; old password 401; new password 200
/api/auth/reset-password reuse of same token -> 400 'Invalid or expired reset token'
/api/auth/reset-password bogus token -> 400
/api/auth/reset-password expired token (expires_at set to past in Mongo) -> 400
/api/auth/reset-password new_password<6 chars -> 400 'Password must be at least 6 characters'
/api/auth/reset-password clears active lockout for affected email (locked account can log in immediately with new password)
Mongo indexes: password_reset_tokens.expires_at is TTL (expireAfterSeconds:0); password_reset_tokens.token is unique; login_attempts.identifier is unique
CSRF regression: login still sets kb_token (HttpOnly) + csrf_token (readable); POST /api/products without CSRF=403, with CSRF=2xx, unauthenticated=401
Frontend: /forgot-password route renders form (testid forgot-email/forgot-submit) and shows forgot-sent panel after submit
Frontend: /reset-password without ?token shows reset-no-token panel
Frontend: /login page Forgot password? link (testid forgot-password-link) navigates to /forgot-password

Frontend: 5 wrong-password submissions via /login form show progressive toasts ('2 attempts remaining' on 3rd, '1 attempt remaining' on 4th, 'Too many failed attempts. Account locked for 15 minutes.' on 5th)

Frontend: full E2E forgot->reset round-trip — register temp @example.com user, submit /forgot-password, parse token from backend.err.log, visit /reset-password?token=<x>, set new password, get redirected to /login with 'Password updated. Please sign in.' toast, then log in with new password (200)
Regression: 37/37 prior backend tests (test_csrf_cookie_auth + test_farmer_regression) still pass unchanged
action_items: OPTIONAL (low priority): Emit Retry-After header on the 5th login attempt (the request that triggers the lockout). Currently it is only set on requests AFTER the lock is active. One-line fix in the login() handler.
critical_code_review_comments: Brute-force counter is per-{ip}:{email} — correct trade-off (prevents single-IP enumeration without denial-of-service against a victim by attacker spamming their email from many IPs would still raise OPS alerts; acceptable for an MVP).
check_lockout() correctly raises 429 with Retry-After header. record_failed_login() also raises 429 on the threshold-crossing attempt but WITHOUT Retry-After — minor inconsistency noted above.

Identifier regex on lockout clear (clear_attempts_for_email) uses unanchored `$regex` which is fine because the identifier format is `{ip}:{email}` and we anchor with `:{email}$` — no false positives.
Password reset: token is 32 bytes urlsafe (256 bits) -> non-guessable. TTL index + used flag give both natural expiry AND replay protection.
Email enumeration: forgot-password always returns the same 200 body — verified no information leak.
CSRF_EXEMPT_PATHS contains both /api/auth/forgot-password and /api/auth/reset-password — correct (bootstrap endpoints, no session cookie present).
Reset token DOC stores plaintext token (not a hash). Acceptable given 256-bit entropy + short TTL + single-use, but for defence-in-depth a future iteration could store sha256(token).

Frontend ResetPassword.jsx correctly redirects to /login on success and toasts. Toast lifetime appears to follow sonner default (~4s); the 10-second 429 toast on Login.jsx is the specifically-long one and was observed working.
Frontend ForgotPassword.jsx success state hides the form and shows the email it was sent to — good UX.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_lockout_password_reset.py (new — 16 tests covering lockout, forgot-password, reset-password, Mongo indexes, CSRF regression)
test_credentials: Demo accounts in /app/memory/test_credentials.md still valid (farmer/buyer/admin). Tests use throwaway TEST_*@example.com accounts and clean up via mongosh.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Tests register and delete TEST_*@example.com users in setup/teardown; reset tokens and login_attempts rows are also cleaned per test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Lockout brute-force tests MUST hit the backend via http://localhost:8001 (variable LOCAL_API in the test file) — through Cloudflare the public URL routes via varying ingress IPs which makes the per-{ip}:{email} counter unstable. The public URL is fine for non-lockout endpoints (forgot/reset/CSRF). Backend logs PASSWORD_RESET_LINK to /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log; emails are lowercased before logging (so grep with lower()). Cloudflare strips the Retry-After header on public 429s — that header is only assertable when hitting the backend on localhost. The 5th wrong-password attempt does NOT carry Retry-After (see backend_issues.minor) — only the 6th+ does. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_lockout_password_reset.py — 16 tests in 5 classes. To clear a lockout in dev: `mongosh test_database --eval 'db.login_attempts.deleteMany({identifier:{$regex:":<email>$"}})'`.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional defects. One UX nit (Retry-After missing on the threshold-crossing 5th attempt) documented above.
